rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL structure and insert generated annex content only within the owner scope that matches the prepared contract block.
Do not move structural elements or alter component/interface declarations during fusion.
Rely on the analyzed model as the source of truth for valid owners, scopes, and visible names before merging generated content.
generic_rules: Keep contracts that reference interface features with the component type owner, and keep implementation-only content with the implementation owner.
Preserve all non-AGREE architecture text unchanged except for the minimal scoped insertion needed.
Avoid introducing references that are not valid in the selected owner scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is wrapped in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-scope content should remain after relevant structural declarations when required by scope.
anti_patterns: Do not fuse type-scope contract content into an implementation owner or vice versa.
Do not rewrite architecture, connections, properties, or names while inserting annex content.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property expressions as AGREE variables
